text:
In a small town tucked into the hills of Kentucky, nestled among whispering pines and cobwebbed dollhouses, lived a strange old scientist. Every morning before dawn broke, Professor Armitage would pour himself into a steaming cup of coffee and delve into his latest cryptid research.

One chilly spring morning, desperate to prove that feathered creatures flew and not just some wild fantasy concocted by lonely winter nights, Professor Armitage released a flock of swallows into an open cage door. Outside the cage fluffed tiny feathers and fluttered about in delight.

Back inside his cluttered laboratory, armed with binoculars and a keen eye for detail, Professor Armitage watched as the birds circled.

Within a dozen minutes, a figure emerged from the depths of the forest. It was a creature unlike any beast seen in any biologist's field journal. Large, feathered wings flapping wildly. Four wings in all.

Professor Armitage was unsure how to classify this
